nameMeanings.name: Castell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Castille
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Antoine, Armand, Maudry, Michel, Monique, Normand, Remy, Renella.
Americanized form of Catalan   Castell  , under the French influence in LA.
French: habitational name from the French name for Castile in Spain (see   Castilla  ).

variants.name: Castle
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Castell was born in 1510, in England, his father, Sir Edmund John Payne, was 48 and his mother, Elizabeth Walton, was 22. He married Constance in 1530, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He was buried in Somersham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Castell est né en 1510, à Angleterre, son père, Sir Edmund John Payne, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Walton, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Constance en 1530, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Ilenterré à Somersham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Castell 1510 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edmund John Payne 48 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Walton 22. Er hat 1530 in England Constance geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er wurde in Somersham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Castell è nato il nel 1510 nel England, il suo padre, Sir Edmund John Payne, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Walton, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Constance nel 1530, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Fu sepolto a Somersham, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Castellが1510年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir Edmund John Payneは48歳，母Elizabeth Waltonは22歳であった。1530年にイギリスでConstanceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアソメルシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Castell(이)가 1510년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edmund John Payne(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Walton(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1530년 영국에서 Constance(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 서머샤에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Constance em 1530, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele foi sepultado em Somersham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Castell родился в 1510 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edmund John Payne было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Walton, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance в 1530 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности сомиршам, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.
